content: The 27th Amendment prohibits any law that increases or decreases the salary of members of Congress from taking effect until after the next election of representatives. This means that Congress cannot give itself a pay raise or cut its pay without voters having a chance to weigh in at the polls first.
raw_content: The 27th Amendment prohibits any law that increases or decreases the salary of members of Congress from taking effect until after the next election of representatives.
